title: Kubernetes 1.29 New Features
script: Here's your briefing on the major features introduced in Kubernetes 1.29, codenamed Mandala. This release focuses on stability, performance, and developer experience improvements. One of the headline features is the stable release of ReadWriteOncePod access mode for persistent volumes. This ensures that only one pod across the entire cluster can mount a volume for read-write access, which is crucial for applications requiring exclusive access to storage. The KMS v2 API has graduated to stable, providing enhanced encryption capabilities for etcd data with better performance and key rotation features. This is a significant security improvement for cluster operators managing sensitive workloads. For networking, the Service Traffic Distribution feature is now in beta, allowing more granular control over how traffic is distributed to service endpoints. This enables better load balancing strategies and improved performance for latency-sensitive applications. The Pod Ready++ feature has also reached beta status, providing more sophisticated pod readiness checks that consider additional factors beyond just container readiness. In terms of storage, CSI volume expansion is now more reliable with improved error handling and rollback capabilities. The device plugin framework has been enhanced to support more complex resource allocation patterns. Node management gets better with the Node Lifecycle Controller improvements, providing more predictable behavior during node maintenance and failures. Developer experience improvements include better kubectl output formatting, enhanced debugging capabilities, and more informative error messages. The API priority and fairness system has been refined to provide better request handling under high load conditions. This release also includes numerous bug fixes and performance optimizations across the entire codebase. Kubernetes 1.29 represents another step forward in platform maturity and reliability.
